description_long: This object consists of several cylindrical and tubular components, each with metallic flanges and fittings, arranged in a way that suggests modular assembly. The main body is a long, straight tube with a flared end, accompanied by a shorter tube, an elbow-shaped segment, and a few smaller cylindrical parts that could represent a bolt or sight. The surfaces are smooth and metallic, with a light gray finish and subtle signs of wear or industrial use. The overall construction appears robust, with visible seams and attachment points typical of machined metal. The asset lacks any visible wooden or polymer furniture, and there are no clear ergonomic features such as a pistol grip or stock. The design is utilitarian, with a focus on function over aesthetics, and the components are likely made of steel or a similar alloy.
description: A modular, metallic rifle-like object composed of several cylindrical and tubular parts with flanges and fittings, finished in light gray. The design is industrial and robust, lacking ergonomic features or decorative elements.
description_view_0: The view shows the circular end of the main tube prominently, with the other components aligned in the background, highlighting the flared flange and the overall cylindrical geometry.
description_view_1: This side view displays the full length of the main tube, the elbow segment, and the smaller parts, making the modular assembly and attachment points more visible.
description_view_2: From this angle, the arrangement of the tubes and fittings is clear, with the main tube receding into the background and the smaller components in the foreground.
description_view_3: This view emphasizes the length of the main tube and the alignment of all parts, showing the flanges and the industrial finish of the surfaces.
